originally [/kəˈniːsəs/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"), now more commonly [/kəˈniːʃəs/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"), is a [town](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_New_York#Town "Administrative divisions of New York") in [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York"), [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), United States. The population was 2,473 at the 2010 census.[[3]](#cite_note-Census_2010-3) The name is said to be derived from a [Seneca](/wiki/Seneca_language "Seneca language") word, *Ga-ne-a-sos*, translated as “place of nanny berries"[[4]](#cite_note-4)

**Conesus,** originally [/kəˈniːsəs/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"), now more commonly [/kəˈniːʃəs/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"), is a [town](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_New_York#Town "Administrative divisions of New York") in [Livingston County](/wiki/Livingston_County,_New_York "Livingston County, New York"), [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), United States. The population was 2,473 at the 2010 census.[[3]](#cite_note-Census_2010-3) The name is said to be derived from a [Seneca](/wiki/Seneca_language "Seneca language") word, *Ga-ne-a-sos*, translated as “place of nanny berries"[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Conesus is on the county's east border, is bordered in part by [Conesus Lake](/wiki/Conesus_Lake "Conesus Lake") and [Hemlock Lake](/wiki/Hemlock_Lake "Hemlock Lake"), and is north of [Dansville](/wiki/Dansville,_Livingston_County,_New_York "Dansville, Livingston County, New York").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Conesus,_New_York&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Settlement began *circa* 1795. In 1819, Conesus was formed from parts of the towns of [Groveland](/wiki/Groveland,_New_York "Groveland, New York") and [Livonia](/wiki/Livonia_(town),_New_York "Livonia (town), New York") as the town Freeport. The name was changed to "Bowersville" in March 1825 and was changed to "Conesus" a month later.

Conesus is home to the former St. Michael's Mission, a school for Catholic priests that operated from the 1930s until the 1970s. The sprawling complex on Mission Road is now owned by an evangelical Christian church from [Colorado Springs](/wiki/Colorado_Springs "Colorado Springs").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The town is also the location of O-Neh-Da Vineyard, which has roots back to 1872 and is one of the last wineries in the country to make [sacramental wine](/wiki/Sacramental_wine "Sacramental wine") from their own [vineyards](/wiki/Vineyard "Vineyard").

The [Conesus Amusement Hall](/wiki/Conesus_Amusement_Hall "Conesus Amusement Hall"), [Kellerman Log Cabin](/wiki/Kellerman_Log_Cabin "Kellerman Log Cabin"), [Payne Cobblestone House](/wiki/Payne_Cobblestone_House "Payne Cobblestone House"), and [Sliker Cobblestone House](/wiki/Sliker_Cobblestone_House "Sliker Cobblestone House") are listed on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places").[[6]](#cite_note-nris-6)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0c/Buliding_Formerly_Known_as_St._Michael%27s_Mission.jpg/250px-Buliding_Formerly_Known_as_St._Michael%27s_Mission.jpg)](/wiki/File:Buliding_Formerly_Known_as_St._Michael%27s_Mission.jpg)

**Building Formerly Known as St. Michael's Mission in 2024.**

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Conesus,_New_York&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the town has a total area of 35.9 square miles (92.9 km2), of which 32.9 square miles (85.2 km2) are land and 3.0 square miles (7.7 km2), or 8.31%, are water.[[7]](#cite_note-Gazetteer_2019-7)

[Conesus Lake](/wiki/Conesus_Lake "Conesus Lake") is on the west border of the town, and [Hemlock Lake](/wiki/Hemlock_Lake "Hemlock Lake") is on the east border of the town. Both lakes are considered minor [Finger Lakes](/wiki/Finger_Lakes "Finger Lakes"). The east town line is the border of [Ontario County](/wiki/Ontario_County,_New_York "Ontario County, New York"). Several communities, including Sunny Shores, Excelsior Springs, and Walkleys Landing, are located on the east shore of Conesus Lake in the town. There is no housing along the shore of Hemlock Lake, because it is owned by the city of [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York") for its water supply.

[New York State Route 15](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15 "New York State Route 15") is an important north-south highway, which intersects the former [New York State Route 255](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_255 "New York State Route 255") at Conesus village.

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Conesus,_New_York&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Demographics")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e2/Conesus%2C_New_York_town_hall.JPG/250px-Conesus%2C_New_York_town_hall.JPG)](/wiki/File:Conesus,_New_York_town_hall.JPG)

[Conesus town hall](/wiki/Conesus_Amusement_Hall "Conesus Amusement Hall")

As of the [census](/wiki/Census "Census")[[9]](#cite_note-GR2-9) of 2000, there were 2,353 people, 869 households, and 662 families residing in the town. The population density was 71.5 inhabitants per square mile (27.6/km2). There were 983 housing units at an average density of 29.9 per square mile (11.5/km2). The racial makeup of the town was 98.43% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 0.17% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 0.34% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 0.04% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.13% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), and 0.89% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 0.21% of the population.

There were 869 households, out of which 38.7% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 63.6% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 7.9% had a female householder with no husband present, and 23.8% were non-families. 18.2% of all households were made up of individuals, and 7.1% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.69 and the average family size was 3.05.

In the town, the population was spread out, with 27.4% under the age of 18, 6.2% from 18 to 24, 31.0% from 25 to 44, 25.8% from 45 to 64, and 9.6% who were 65 years of age or older. The median age was 38 years. For every 100 females, there were 97.7 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 96.4 males.

The median income for a household in the town was $48,200, and the median income for a family was $53,125. Males had a median income of $40,313 versus $25,000 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the town was $22,773. About 3.9% of families and 5.3% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 7.0% of those under age 18 and 3.5% of those age 65 or over.

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Conesus,_New_York&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Andrew Kuder](/wiki/Andrew_Kuder "Andrew Kuder"), soldier who fought in the [American Civil War](/wiki/American_Civil_War "American Civil War").
* [Frederick D. Losey](/wiki/Frederick_D._Losey "Frederick D. Losey"), [Shakespearian](/wiki/Shakespearian "Shakespearian") scholar and [elocutionist](/wiki/Elocutionist "Elocutionist")
* [Daniel Shays](/wiki/Daniel_Shays "Daniel Shays"), a leader of [Shays' Rebellion](/wiki/Shays%27_Rebellion "Shays' Rebellion")[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Communities and locations in Conesus

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Conesus,_New_York&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Communities and locations in Conesus")]

* **[Conesus](/wiki/Conesus_(CDP),_New_York "Conesus (CDP), New York")** – The [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(place) "Hamlet (place)") of Conesus on NY-15.
* **Conesus Inlet** – A stream draining into the south part of Conesus Lake.
* **Conesus Inlet Fish and Wildlife Management Area** – A conservation area at the south end of Conesus Lake.
* **Crocketts Corners** – A location north of Conesus village on NY-15.
* **Excelsior Springs** – A location on Conesus Lake, north of Walkleys Landing.
* **Foots Corners** – A hamlet north of Conesus village on NY-15.
* **McMillan Creek** – A stream flowing through Conesus village and terminating in Conesus Lake.
* **Sunny Shores** – A location on the north town line by Conesus Lake.
* **Union Corners** – A former community in the town.
* **Walkleys Landing** – A hamlet at the south end of Conesus Lake.
* **Websters Crossing** – A hamlet by the south town line on NY-15 and McMillan Creek.

All of the hamlets listed on the shore of Conesus Lake are part of the [Conesus Lake](/wiki/Conesus_Lake,_New_York "Conesus Lake, New York") [census-designated place](/wiki/Census-designated_place "Census-designated place").
